title: E-Commerce Platform
description: Enterprise-grade e-commerce platform with an Angular 19 storefront and admin dashboard, a Quarkus (Java 21) REST API, and a fully dockerized PostgreSQL 16 backend.
architecture: An Angular 19 standalone storefront and admin dashboard talk to a Quarkus REST API (JAX-RS resources → services → Panache repositories → JPA entities) backed by PostgreSQL 16, with Flyway as the single source of truth for schema migrations across both the standalone Flyway container and Quarkus' built-in Flyway. Guest carts merge into the account cart on login via an X-Cart-Session header and a dedicated merge endpoint.
challenges: Keeping guest and authenticated carts consistent, and keeping historical orders accurate after products are edited or deleted.
solutions: Carts key off either a user_id or a session_id with partial unique indexes, enabling guest carts server-side with a clean merge on login. Orders snapshot addresses as JSONB and order_items snapshot product name, SKU and price, so historical orders survive catalog changes.
features: JWT auth with rotating refresh tokens and guest-to-account cart merge
Admin dashboard: product, category, coupon and order management, plus customer activation
Full-text product search with GIN indexes and denormalized ratings
EN/DE i18n, installable PWA with an offline fallback, CI/CD to Render and Vercel
lessons: Immutable order snapshots — JSONB addresses, denormalized order-item pricing — are worth the extra write complexity; they're what let historical orders survive catalog changes cleanly.

title: Finance Manager
description: A premium full-stack personal finance platform — transactions, budgets, saving pots, recurring bills and smart insights — built with Next.js 15 and a Quarkus (Java 21) API.
architecture: A Next.js 15 App Router frontend (typed API client with automatic token refresh, TanStack Query) talks over REST + JWT to a layered Quarkus API (resource → service → repository → entity) backed by a normalized PostgreSQL schema with Flyway migrations, composite indexes on hot query paths, and CTEs/window functions powering the analytics endpoints.
challenges: Detecting recurring bills and unusual spending reliably from raw transaction history without any manual tagging.
solutions: Recurring bills are auto-detected statistically: merchants appearing in 3+ distinct months with amount variance under 10% are flagged as bills. Unusual-expense detection flags transactions over 3x a category's rolling average.
features: AG Grid transaction table with server-side paging and CSV import with duplicate detection
Budgets with live spend tracking and color-coded status
Goal-based saving pots with deposits, withdrawals and milestone notifications
Auto-detected recurring bills with daily email reminders
Dashboard with a financial health score, cash-flow chart and spending insights
lessons: A seeded, idempotent demo account with months of realistic data makes a portfolio project reviewable in one click — worth building before polishing any single feature further.
